mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending corporate earnings contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting higher corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side momentum and positive earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO month-over-month changes are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations during easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in consumer price inflation and the federal funds rate signal aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor market expansion and rising aggregate income, supporting corporate earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.01 over 5-year rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent quarter-over-quarter signals demand exhaustion and recession risk, prompting defensive portfolio reallocation that generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 70% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales confirm immediate economic momentum, driving positive short-term risk appetite and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Co-movement in INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict positive 5-day returns (hit rate < 52% over out-of-sample testing).
